output: Batch 8 validation completed. The ordered pinned-group verification gates passed, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, the full `src/tools/video-editor/` Vitest suite passed in this worktree, and the throwaway repro script confirmed cohesion holds across legacy migration, drag, resize, and sync-style child replacement before being deleted.
deviations: The batch-8 checkpoint file did not already exist, so this batch created it.

Running the throwaway repro via `npx tsx tmp-pinned-group-repro.ts` failed in the sandbox because the tsx launcher could not bind its IPC pipe (`listen EPERM ... .pipe`). The same script succeeded via `node --import tsx tmp-pinned-group-repro.ts`, and the script was then deleted.
The previously noted unrelated TimelineCanvas pinned-group failure did not reproduce in this worktree; the full `src/tools/video-editor/` suite passed outright.

task_updates.executor_notes: Ran the validation sequence in order: the dedicated projection and cohesion modules passed, the updated existing regression set passed, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and the full `src/tools/video-editor/` Vitest suite passed in this worktree. The full-suite run emitted only non-failing diagnostics already seen earlier: `repairConfig` info logs, the `ClipAction.test.tsx` React `act(...)` warning, the `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` `[PERF] render-storm` diagnostic, and expected `DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx` thrown-error stderr from its error-path assertions. Added a short temporary repro script that loaded a legacy pinned group through `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry`, then exercised `moveGroup`, `resizeGroupEdge`, and `replaceGroupChildren`; it confirmed cohesion held throughout and was deleted after the successful run.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, the full `src/tools/video-editor/` Vitest suite passed in this worktree, and the throwaway repro script confirmed cohesion held across legacy migration, drag, resize, and sync-style child replacement.
